label: SGT James Williams — Army, Afghanistan (OEF), Bad Conduct Discharge, PTSD, Tinnitus, Back Pain

dd214.name: Williams, James D.
dd214.decorations: Afghanistan Campaign Medal, Combat Infantryman Badge, National Defense Service Medal
dd214.dutyLocations: Fort Benning GA, Forward Operating Base Salerno Afghanistan, Combat Outpost Saro Afghanistan, Fort Carson CO
dd214.remarks: Served two deployments to Afghanistan (OEF 2011, OEF 2012-2013). Infantry squad leader during active combat operations Kunar and Paktika provinces. Separated under characterization of Bad Conduct Discharge per administrative review board decision in 2014.

vaRecords.provider: VA Eastern Colorado Health Care System
vaRecords.notes: Veteran reports nightmares, hypervigilance, and avoidance behaviors related to Afghanistan service. Tinnitus began after exposure to IEDs and sustained weapons fire. Lower back pain attributed to heavy load carries during deployments. PCL-5 score: 52.

Pure tone audiometry confirms bilateral sensorineural hearing loss with prominent tinnitus in right ear. Pattern consistent with noise-induced hearing loss from combat exposures. Hearing aids recommended.

MRI shows early degenerative changes at L4-L5 with mild disc protrusion. Consistent with mechanical low back pain from heavy field load and repetitive impact during military operations. Physical therapy initiated.

currentMedications: Ibuprofen as needed for back pain
summary.functionalImpact: PTSD symptoms limit social engagement. Tinnitus disrupts sleep. Back pain limits sitting tolerance to 20-30 minutes.
